schema_notes: Reference implementation for per-billionaire record. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events are canonically scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment). Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream, not in this file.
generated_by: scrooge v3 manual reference run — single-subject deep research

person.wealth_source: Bridgewater Associates — founder 1975, world's largest hedge fund at peak (~$124B AUM). Sold his last shares of Bridgewater in July 2025 for ~$6.8B cash. Remaining wealth is diversified across cash, family office holdings, and real estate.

net_worth.range_note: Forbes 2026 profile rank #188 on the global billionaires list. Bloomberg Billionaires Index Feb 2026 estimates $20B at rank #128. Several aggregators report a wider $14-28B range reflecting uncertainty about cash position and family office holdings after the 2025 Bridgewater exit. Fell within the $15-20B range flagged in scope.

sources.note: Direct Forbes profile fetch blocked in session; rank/value confirmed via aggregator summaries of Forbes 2026 data

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Dalio sold his remaining Bridgewater equity in July 2025 for ~$6.8B cash (per Wikipedia/ValueWalk). The resulting portfolio is much more liquid than a typical tech-founder billionaire — primarily cash, public securities, and family-office holdings rather than concentrated founder stock. Apply 70% liquid for 'giving capacity now.'

cited_events.recipient: Various (recipients not fully itemized in ProPublica summary)
cited_events.notes: Charitable disbursements from Dalio Foundation Inc 2024 990-PF.

cited_events.donor_entity: Dalio Philanthropies (likely flowed through Dalio Foundation Inc)
cited_events.recipient: NewYork-Presbyterian Hospital — Dalio Center for Health Justice
cited_events.dedup_note: Likely captured within 2020 Dalio Foundation grant_out ($33.6M) if paid from the foundation, but more likely spread across multiple years or paid from a separate personal/family-fund vehicle. Leave as standalone direct_gift and flag for dedup when Dalio Foundation 2020 grant recipient list is parsed. If double-counted, subtract from direct_gifts.

cited_events.amount_usd_dalio_share_note: Assume 50% split absent disclosure of the actual ratio; standalone event until the joint-pledge share is verified.
cited_events.dedup_note: Joint with Bloomberg. Dalio share likely recorded through Dalio Foundation grants in subsequent years. Currently flagged as a pledge; roll into grants_out when specific payment years are identified.

cited_events.recipient: Partnership for Connecticut (joint with State of Connecticut)
cited_events.status_note: Partnership dissolved June 2020 less than one year after formation due to governance/transparency issues and leadership crisis. Reported that Dalio had contributed ~$20M at dissolution time, which was refunded or returned to foundation per some reporting. Actual disbursement to partnership uncertain.

cited_events.recipient: ~300,000 Connecticut children — seeding federal Trump Account child investment accounts with $250 each
cited_events.classification_note: Press framed as philanthropy — Dalio Philanthropies seeded children's investment accounts. CNBC/CT Mirror note the accounts were created by the 2025 Trump administration legislation; they are tax-advantaged savings accounts for children, not partisan gifts. Count under observable pledges once payment starts. For now, pledge only.

cited_events.amount_usd_note: $5M plus donation of personal research yacht and equipment (value not separately disclosed).

cited_events.dedup_note: This $280M lifetime CT figure OVERLAPS with Dalio Foundation grants_out. Use for narrative context only. DO NOT add to observable rollup (would double-count).

pledges_and_announcements.status_source: IPS Giving Pledge at 15 (July 2025) — Dalio's direct-to-charity giving is ongoing and at a materially higher rate than typical signers.
pledges_and_announcements.pledge_letter_quote: We had planned to give most of our money to those it will most help anyway. For all of these reasons, we enthusiastically make this giving pledge.
pledge_focus_areas: Joint: meditation programs for inner-city school children and stressed communities
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Pledge committed to giving 'more than half' of wealth in lifetime. Dalio's net worth at 2011 signing was ~$10B — 50% target in 2011 dollars ~$5B. Current net worth ~$20B; 50% target now ~$10B. With ~$1B+ already distributed in grants from Dalio Foundation (per Wikipedia) and $7B+ contributed to Dalio Philanthropies vehicles, pledge is notably more on-track than most signers.

political_giving.substitution_note: Political giving is minimal; does not displace charitable giving at a material level.
political_giving.observed_total_note: Dalio has publicly self-identified as 'apolitical' and 'a strong bipartisan moderate' (LinkedIn, 2023). Last publicly known direct political donation was $5,000 in 1998 to Freedom & Free Enterprise PAC. Political giving is not a material contributor to his philanthropic profile. Placeholder $50K upper bound pending FEC bulk pull. Political giving is NOT a red flag for Dalio.

sources.note: Dalio self-described as apolitical bipartisan moderate

foundations_active.name: Dalio Foundation Inc
foundations_active.officers_compensation: ZERO_COMP most years (Ray Dalio, Barbara Dalio, Matthew Dalio, Janine Racanelli, Lisa Safian). Exception: 2014 and 2015 filings show $500,000 aggregate officer comp. Effectively zero comp at current scale.
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants out ($59.2M) vs assets ($1.67B) = 3.5% — BELOW the 5% legal minimum. 2023 was 1.1% ($17.2M/$1.54B). 2022 was 0.8% ($12.3M/$1.51B). 2021 was 0.85%. These are multi-year sub-5% payouts. Flag: LOW_PAYOUT. (Note: IRS 5% payout can be satisfied via qualifying distributions that include admin expenses, not just grants paid — verify before publishing.)
foundations_active.red_flag_daf_transfers: NOT_YET_VERIFIED — would require parsing grant recipient list from full 990-PF filings.

foundations_active.name: Dalio Family Fund Inc
foundations_active.classification_note: This is the SECOND Dalio vehicle. It is a public charity (files Form 990, not 990-PF) with $4B+ in assets. Its grantmaking focuses on Education, Philanthropy/Voluntarism/Grantmaking, and Human Services in CT, NY, and CA. Grant-out activity not yet retrieved — THIS IS THE PRIMARY OUTSTANDING DATA GAP.
foundations_active.todo: CRITICAL: Pull Dalio Family Fund Inc 2019-2024 Form 990 filings to capture grants_paid. Likely substantially larger than Dalio Foundation Inc based on $4B asset base vs $1.67B.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No DAF activity publicly identified. Dalio Family Fund's public-charity classification with $4B assets is structurally similar to a DAF sponsor but operates as a family-directed grantmaker. Absence-of-evidence only.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: Dalio Philanthropies is an umbrella brand, not a legal entity — operates through Dalio Foundation Inc, Dalio Family Fund Inc, and the Dalio family office. Program arms include Dalio Education, OceanX, Endless, China Care Foundation, Dalio Center for Health Justice. No philanthropic LLC equivalent to CZI or Emerson Collective.
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2. Bridgewater had prior offshore-vehicle press attention; Dalio personally has not been named in Pandora/Paradise/Panama Papers per public reporting, but not verified.

trusts_disclosed_existence_structure_unk.source_quote: Dalio Family Office operates as family LP allocator and manages Dalio wealth post-Bridgewater
trusts_disclosed_existence_structure_unk.classification_note: Post-2025 Bridgewater exit, wealth is managed through Dalio Family Office. Specific trust/entity structure for charitable commitments not disclosed publicly. Per Giving Pledge letter, intent is to give 'more than half' but disbursement vehicle structure is not public.

program_arms_under_dalio_philanthropies.name: Dalio Education
program_arms_under_dalio_philanthropies.focus: Connecticut public school funding, youth programs
program_arms_under_dalio_philanthropies.note: Primary vehicle after Partnership for CT dissolution in 2020

program_arms_under_dalio_philanthropies.name: Dalio Center for Health Justice (at NewYork-Presbyterian)

red_flags.evidence: Dalio Foundation Inc grants_paid as % of assets was 3.5% (2024), 1.1% (2023), 0.8% (2022), 0.85% (2021). All below the 5% IRS minimum at face value. IRS 5% test includes admin expenses so may be satisfied formally, but grant-out cadence is notably slow relative to $1.67B asset base.

Partnership for Connecticut ($100M pledge, 2019) dissolved June 2020 less than one year after launch due to transparency issues — state partners exempted the entity from FOIA at Dalio family insistence. Leadership crisis: CEO Mary Anne Schmitt-Carey fired after six weeks by Barbara Dalio et al.; subsequent defamation/breach-of-contract lawsuit filed. Bipartisan Connecticut legislative criticism.

Dalio operates both a private foundation (Dalio Foundation Inc, $1.67B assets) and a public charity (Dalio Family Fund Inc, $4.04B assets). Flow of funds between entities, and from Dalio personally into each, is not yet mapped. Public-charity structure can function like a DAF sponsor without DAF-level disclosure; warrants review.

red_flags_not_present: GIVING_PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED (Dalio is progressing materially — $1B+ already disbursed, $7B+ contributed to vehicles)
POLITICAL_DOMINATES_CHARITABLE (political giving is negligible — self-described apolitical)
HIGH_OFFICER_COMP (zero comp most years across both foundations)
FOR_PROFIT_PIVOT (no for-profit hybrid vehicle like EIT)

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(Dalio Foundation 990-PF grants_out for years we have: 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024) = $44.5M + $30.1M + $110.5M + $121.6M + $71.5M + $58.5M + $33.6M + $12.6M + $12.3M + $17.2M + $59.2M = $571.6M. Plus Woods Hole 2012 ($5M, likely not through foundation) + OceanX Dalio share estimate partially attributed elsewhere. Simplification: $571.6M foundation total + $10M non-foundation direct gifts floor = ~$583.5M. CRITICAL GAP: Dalio Family Fund Inc (EIN 83-1575536, $4B assets) grants_out are NOT included — that entity likely accounts for the gap between this $583M and the $1B+ figure cited by Wikipedia and the $7B+ family-level figure cited by Dalio Philanthropies.
observable_giving_todo: CRITICAL: Pull Dalio Family Fund Inc (EIN 83-1575536) Form 990 for 2019-2024. This is likely the primary giving vehicle and is currently missing from this rollup.
Pull Dalio Foundation 990-PF years 2003-2010 and 2016-2018 to close pre-current-window and gap-year coverage.
Dedup Dalio Center for Health Justice $50M 2020 gift against Dalio Foundation 2020 grant recipients list when retrieved.
Dedup Partnership for CT $100M 2019 pledge against Dalio Foundation 2019 grant recipients (reported partial disbursement before 2020 dissolution).
Verify OceanX funding allocations — confirm whether joint Bloomberg partnership $185M figure reflects pledges paid or announced-only.
Run SEC EDGAR Form 4 for Bridgewater CIKs — check for stock gifts (less likely post-exit but possible 2015-2024 history).
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.70 × $20B = $14.0B; tenure cap saturated (14 years). Expected = $1.4B. This is a STRICT denominator matching the formal 10% norm. Expected is much lower than for Ellison because Dalio is less wealthy; expected ratio becomes correspondingly more achievable.

hidden_upper_usd.daf_uplift_note: No DAF evidence. Zero uplift from DAFs specifically.
hidden_upper_usd.family_fund_uplift_note: Dalio Family Fund Inc (EIN 83-1575536) has $4.04B assets and is a public charity with disclosed-but-unretrieved Form 990 filings. Based on typical public-charity grantmaking payout of ~8-15% on endowed accounts and the explicitly stated 'Dalio Philanthropies has granted over $7B' lifetime figure, estimate $2B of lifetime grants from this entity not yet in observable. This should CONVERT to observable once 990s are parsed.
hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked (Phase 2). Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of older HNW donors give anonymously sometimes. Dalio has not signaled a preference for anonymity (in fact, aggressively named) but applying cohort-median 0.2% of liquid wealth = 0.002 × $20B × 0.70 = $28M, rounded to $50M conservative upper.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: No publicly disclosed religious affiliation with tithing norms. Zero.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: Dalio family office structure and any charitable trust commitments not disclosed. Given $20B current net worth and ~$10B pledge target, apply $500M uncertainty envelope to represent undisbursed charitable trust assets that could plausibly be deployed over next 10 years.
hidden_upper_usd.total_method: family_fund + anonymous + trust (daf/llc/offshore/religious all zero). NOTE: the family_fund component is NOT truly hidden — it's in publicly filed 990s; it's just not yet retrieved in this research pass. Once retrieved, it should move from hidden_upper to observable.

rollup.tier_rule: ON_TRACK requires (observable + hidden_upper) / expected > 1.0 AND pledge progress visible. Dalio passes: observable_plus_hidden_ratio = 2.24 (substantially above 1.0). Observable-only ratio is 0.42 (below 1.0) but the Dalio Family Fund Inc omission materially understates observable — once that $4B-asset entity's grants_out are retrieved, observable should rise to ~$2-3B range, putting observable-only ratio at 1.5-2.0. Dalio is the canonical example of a SIGNED PLEDGER WHO IS ACTUALLY GIVING — contrast with Ellison (Tier B) and Zuckerberg (Tier C/D).
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Readers should see Dalio as a signed Giving Pledge member who is materially delivering on the commitment. Key nuances: (1) the $7B+ 'Dalio Philanthropies' lifetime figure includes transfers INTO the foundations and family fund, not just grants OUT — actual charitable deployment is lower. (2) LOW_PAYOUT flag on Dalio Foundation Inc 2021-2024 is real — the foundation has ballooned from $740M to $1.67B in assets while grant cadence slowed. (3) Connecticut partnership governance collapse in 2020 is a genuine governance flag. (4) Observable giving is understated in this file because Dalio Family Fund Inc 990s are not yet retrieved.
rollup.expected_5pct_tenure_method: 5% × net_worth_best × liquidity_pct × years_as_billionaire (uncapped tenure); computed by aggregate_v3.py
rollup.observable_reconciliation_note: Published `observable_giving_usd` reflects agent judgment about attributable giving. `observable_from_events_usd` is the strict sum of canonical grant_out + direct_gift event amounts. A gap can mean self-reported lifetime figures beyond cited events (rollup > sum) or exclusion of inherited-foundation / family-foundation portions (rollup < sum).

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: v2 legacy formula. DO NOT PUBLISH. For continuity with existing pipeline dashboards only. Lower than Ellison (98) because Dalio is actually giving, and much lower than Buffett's 58 is not comparable since Dalio has less tenure.
